text: Chapter 6 is dominated by notated exercises (arpeggios, scalar runs, and chord forms across the altered dominant Types 1, 2, and 3 in fingerboard Areas 2-4) with brief marginal prose directives rather than expository passages. The load-bearing prose consists of standing practice rules: a substitution allowance (A7+ for A7#9+, D49 for D6/9) that applies across all subsequent Type 3 pages, instructions to finger Em9 runs in the 5th position as well, a note that arpeggio tones can move to the 5th and 4th strings, and a left-hand technique reminder against tip-of-finger placement. Greene's characteristic move visible in the chapter is the bracketed, hand-annotated style of pedagogy — terse rules attached to specific exercises rather than discursive explanation — which assumes the framework established in earlier chapters and trusts the student to apply it. No new theoretical terminology is introduced; the chapter is the practical realization of the Overtone Dominant / Group-3 framework defined earlier.
